title: Helenium alatum
taxonomy: Helenium alatum is a flowering plant classified in the family Asteraceae. It is further grouped into the order Asterales, class Magnoliopsida, and division Magnoliophyta. The species belongs to the genus Helenium. Helenium alatum is native to the prairies and open woods of the central United States. It is commonly known as the winged sneezeweed or winged false sunflower.
uses: Helenium alatum, also known as winged sneezeweed, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's roots and leaves are said to have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. Its antiseptic properties are believed to have been used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Helenium alatum for medicinal purposes.

constituents: Helenium alatum contains sesquiterpene lactones, such as alatin and 11β-hydroxyalatin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Additionally, the plant is also known to contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its potential health benefits. Research suggests that the sesquiterpene lactones in Helenium alatum may also have antimicrobial properties. Further studies are needed to fully understand the active constituents of this plant and their effects on human health.
parts: Helenium alatum is a perennial plant that is native to North America. The medicinal parts of Helenium alatum, also known as Sneezeweed, are primarily its flowers, leaves, and roots. The flowers and leaves are said to have a cooling effect on the skin, making them useful for treating fever and skin irritations. intro: Helenium alatum, also known as winged sneezeweed, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to the Great Plains region of North America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in alleviating symptoms of respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Helenium alatum include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Helenium alatum is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often employed in traditional medicine to support immune function and soothe respiratory issues.
